headline: Transcript: Will IPOs ever come back?
summary: Katie Martin speaks to Craig Coben, a contributing writer for FT Alphaville
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Unhedged podcast episode: ‘Will IPOs ever come back?’

Katie MartinOK, listen up because those shiny bankers with their fancy suits and their good hair really mean it this time. Next year is gonna be a biggie for new companies hitting stock markets. Stocks are looking rosy. A whole bunch of companies and their private equity backers have been itching for their moment to launch and those bankers are raring to go and get the deals out there.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

So today on the show, we’re asking, firstly, really? I’ve heard this before and it just never happens. We’re also asking whodunnit? Why have we had a slow patch in this stuff recently? And we’re asking, aside from the bankers who get lovely bonuses from all this, who cares?

This is Unhedged, the markets and finance show from the Financial Times and Pushkin. I’m Katie Martin, a markets columnist and still a baker of delicious mince pies at the FT in London. And I’m joined today by a recovering bank
